title: Handle signals for clean shutdown
description: Trap SIGINT/SIGTERM to flush output, remove temp files, and reap child processes so an interrupt never leaves corruption.
body: Use when a CLI writes files, spawns children, or holds resources. Do not ignore termination signals or leave partial files on Ctrl-C, and do not orphan child processes. Actions: install handlers for SIGINT/SIGTERM that flush buffers, delete temp/partial files (or write atomically via temp+rename), terminate or wait on children, then exit with the conventional 128+signal code. Validation signals include sending SIGINT mid-run and finding no half-written output and no orphaned children. Risk boundary: a tool that leaves a half-written output file on interrupt can corrupt a pipeline's input or a user's data — write atomically and clean up on signal.
